files.contents:
# truncate-middle

A tiny TypeScript library providing a single function: truncateMiddle.

What it does
- Truncates a string by replacing the middle with an ellipsis (or custom marker) when the string is longer than a given maximum length.

Quick start
1. npm install
2. npm test

See SPEC.md for the function specification and examples.

# Function specification: truncateMiddle

Purpose
- Provide a small, deterministic utility to truncate long strings by replacing their middle portion with an ellipsis (or a custom marker), preserving the start and end of the original string.

Function signature (TypeScript)

- truncateMiddle(text: string, maxLength: number, options?: { ellipsis?: string }): string

Parameters
- text: string — the input string to truncate.
- maxLength: number — the maximum allowed length of the returned string. Must be a non-negative integer.
- options.ellipsis?: string — optional replacement inserted in the middle when truncation occurs. Defaults to a single-character ellipsis "…".

Behavior
- If text.length <= maxLength, return text unchanged.
- If maxLength is not a finite integer or is negative, throw a RangeError.
- The returned string must have length <= maxLength.
- If maxLength is less than the length of the provided ellipsis, return the ellipsis truncated to maxLength characters (i.e. ellipsis.slice(0, maxLength)).
- When truncation is required and maxLength is large enough to contain the ellipsis, allocate the remaining characters (maxLength - ellipsis.length) between the start and end of the original string. If there's an odd number, the extra character goes to the start.

Examples
- truncateMiddle("abcdefg", 7) -> "abcdefg" (no change)
- truncateMiddle("abcdefghijklmnop", 9) -> "abcd…mnop" (4 start, 1 ellipsis, 4 end)
- truncateMiddle("abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyz", 7, { ellipsis: "..." }) -> "ab...yz"

notes: Install deps and run tests: npm install && npm test
This project uses TypeScript and Vitest. The test files live under tests/ and are included via tsconfig.json.
